title: willughbeia ceylanica
intro: Willughbeia ceylanica, a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, is a rare and endangered species native to Sri Lanka. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce the toxic cardiac glycoside, willughbeine, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Willughbeia ceylanica include its potential to lower blood pressure, treat cardiac arrhythmias, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. However, due to its toxicity and rarity, it is usually prepared in herbal remedies by skilled practitioners, often in the form of a tincture or decoction, and should be used under medical supervision.
